---
title: "RLMJC01: Byrne joint correlated growth — word reading, receptive vocabulary, digit recall"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

RLMJC01 is the joint multivariate extension of the per-measure
historical-growth suite (#338 Phase B): BAS word reading, BPVS receptive
vocabulary and BAS recall of digits fitted **together** over the Byrne cohort,
each with its own supported-cell group-by-wave grid and group-indexed
random-effect scales, and the per-child stable offsets **correlated across
measures**. The headline is the 3×3 between-child correlation matrix of stable
levels — the reading-language-memory coupling question in its most
parsimonious between-child form, and the trajectory-level companion of the
wave-3 measurement model (`lrp-rlm-mm-001`).

::: {.callout-important title="Descriptive natural-history, NOT an intervention effect"}

`readgrp` is a **cohort factor**, not a randomised condition, and every
quantity here is descriptive (`causal_status="none"`). A between-child
correlation of stable levels says nothing about within-child coupling or
direction — the reciprocal reading↔language↔memory question is Phase C's
lagged model, gated on the lagged Byrne DAG.

:::

::: {.callout-note title="Window, frame and assumptions"}

The complete-case core is the suite's waves 1-3, jointly across all three
measures (n = 71 children); waves 4-5 enter as extension waves where a kept
child has **all three** measures observed (wave 5 exists only for the Down
syndrome group). The cross-measure correlation matrix is **shared across the
three reading groups** — a stated parsimony assumption at this n — while the
subject-intercept SDs and overdispersion are group-indexed per the 2026-07-16
heterogeneity decision.

:::

## Model

For child $i$ in group $g[i]$, measure $m$ and wave $t$ (supported cells only):

$$
\text{score}_{imt} \sim \mathrm{BetaBinomial}(N_m,\ p_{imt},\ \kappa_{m,g[i]}),
\qquad
\operatorname{logit}(p_{imt}) = \eta_{m,\,\text{cell}(g[i],t)} + u_{im},
$$

$$
(u_{i1}, u_{i2}, u_{i3}) \sim \mathrm{MVN}\!\left(0,\
\operatorname{diag}(\sigma_{m,g[i]})\, R\,
\operatorname{diag}(\sigma_{m,g[i]})\right),
$$

with $R$ the 3×3 stable-level correlation matrix under an LKJ(2) prior
(non-centred, group-centred per measure for identifiability against the cell
grids). Ceilings: `basread` 90 and `basdig` 34 (confirmed), `bpvs` 32 (Short
Form, confirmed).

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_results_historical_joint.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
